fallbackText: Sentinel — project update. The status dashboard is built and ready; the one blocker to going live is a hosting home (a corporate AWS account).

items.text: PROJECT UPDATE · END USER SERVICES EUROPE
Service Status Dashboard · One real-time view of every service we depend on

body.text: A quick update on the **Sentinel** project — it had been quiet for a couple of months, so here's where it stands. The short version: the build is essentially done. It pulls the operational health of every external service we depend on into a single, real-time view — so during an incident we can answer **"is it them, or is it us?"** at a glance.

Instead of opening a dozen vendor status pages by hand, the team now has one pane of glass over GitHub, AWS, Cloudflare, Microsoft 365, Salesforce, ICE, Snowflake, Datadog and more — alongside our own custom endpoints.

📊 **One pane of glass** — 26 status sources (80 monitored endpoints), grouped by area, with a colour-coded banner

⚡ **Real-time over WebSocket** — the server polls and pushes changes; ten viewers = one call per source, not ten

🔧 **Any source, mostly no code** — built-in adapters (Statuspage, AWS, Azure DevOps, RSS/Atom) plus a visual JSON mapping editor for any API

🧪 **Signal, not noise** — composites roll up the 49 UK Salesforce instances and the 7 ICE feeds; noisy feeds like AWS are scoped to the regions and window we care about

🔒 **Secure by design** — per-source tokens stay server-side, the proxy blocks internal targets (anti-SSRF), and management pages can sit behind a password or SSO

items.text: This is also what has held the project up. Sentinel is a small **always-on server** (background polling + a live WebSocket), so it can't just be "uploaded" somewhere static — it needs somewhere to run. **Without a cloud account to host it — ideally a corporate AWS account — it can't move past a local prototype, which is exactly why it stalled.** A tiny footprint (one container behind a load balancer, fronted by CloudFront, ~$35–80/month) would make it firm-owned and IT-secured. Full architecture written up and available on request.
[https://sentinel.eus.tpicap.com](https://sentinel.eus.tpicap.com) _(target address, once it has a hosting home)_

body.text: _Next up: proactive Teams / email alerts (via Power Automate) the moment a source crosses a chosen severity — so we hear about an outage before a user does._
Developed by Adrien Luce · Paris IT · End User Services Europe
